rf.title: Radio Frequency (RF)
rf.description: RF-related regulations, testing, certification, and export topics.

regulations.title: Regulations
regulations.description: Overview of RF-related regulatory bodies and rules.

subsections.name: Radio Equipment Directive (RED)
subsections.summary: Key EU regulatory framework for radio equipment placed on the market.

entries.brief_summary: The Radio Equipment Directive 2014/53/EU applies to equipment that intentionally emits and/or receives radio waves for communication or radiodetermination.
Equipment must ensure health and safety, electromagnetic compatibility, and effective use of radio spectrum.
Examples include Wi-Fi devices, Bluetooth products, cellular modules, and other wireless transmitters or receivers.

subsections.name: ETSI Harmonized Standards
subsections.summary: Standards commonly used to demonstrate conformity under RED.

entries.brief_summary: Harmonized standards provide a presumption of conformity with relevant RED essential requirements.
Common examples include ETSI EN 300 328 for 2.4 GHz wideband transmission systems and ETSI EN 301 893 for 5 GHz RLAN equipment.
Bundle notes identify the U-Space regulations governing how drones interact with manned aircraft in shared airspace through digital traffic management.
RED establishes the EU market-access framework for radio equipment and sets essential requirements for safety and health, electromagnetic compatibility, and efficient use of radio spectrum.

entries.topic: Geo-awareness and geographical-zone obligations under EU UAS rules
entries.brief_summary: EU open-category rules connect class-marked UAS to active and updated direct remote identification and geo-awareness functions, while operators must obtain geographical-zone information and update geo-awareness data when applicable.

entries.source_title: Bluetooth Core Specification - LE Physical Layer
entries.brief_summary: Bluetooth LE operates in the 2.4 GHz ISM band from 2400 MHz to 2483.5 MHz and uses 40 RF channels spaced 2 MHz apart; relevant when scoping RED/Bluetooth radio behavior for drone modules.

entries.source_title: Bluetooth Core Specification - BR/EDR Radio Specification
entries.brief_summary: Bluetooth Basic Rate uses GFSK modulation in the 2.4 GHz ISM band; useful as a general Bluetooth modulation reference when comparing wireless test scope under RED.

testing.title: Testing
testing.description: Overview of RF testing requirements and procedures.

subsections.name: Radio Frequency Testing
subsections.summary: Radio Frequency testing for UAV systems

entries.topic: Bluetooth modulation modes and channel plans used in RF evaluation
entries.brief_summary: ETC RF deck summarizes Bluetooth modulation modes relevant to RF evaluation: Bluetooth 2.1+EDR uses GFSK, pi/4-DQPSK, and 8-DPSK, while Bluetooth LE uses GFSK; the deck also shows BR/EDR and LE channel-list examples and 20 dB emission-bandwidth examples.

entries.topic: RED RF standards stack for common short-range and broadband wireless devices
entries.brief_summary: ETC RF deck maps the EU RED standards stack, including ETSI EN 300 220, EN 300 330, EN 300 440, EN 300 328, EN 301 893, EN 302 502, EN 301 357, and ERC Recommendation 70-03 for short-range, wideband, and 5 GHz systems.

entries.topic: Drone RF / EMC / LVD test stack for CE entry
entries.source_title: FCC/CE Testing and Certification for Drones - Compliance Testing
entries.brief_summary: Commercial explainer describing CE testing for drones as a stack combining RED for wireless communication, EMC for electromagnetic compatibility, and LVD for battery/power safety, with RF testing across controllers, video, telemetry, GPS, and other links.

emc.title: Electromagnetic Compatibility (EMC)
emc.description: EMC-related regulations, testing, certification, and export topics.

regulations.title: Regulations
regulations.description: Overview of EMC-related rules and standards.

entries.brief_summary: The EMC Directive limits electromagnetic emissions so equipment does not disturb radio, telecommunications, or other equipment when used as intended.
The EMC Directive also governs immunity, aiming to ensure equipment is not disturbed by radio emissions and other electromagnetic interference when used as intended.

entries.topic: ESD as an EMC immunity test family
entries.source_title: EMC Directive 2014/30/EU: Compliance, CE Marking & Testing
entries.brief_summary: Accredited-lab EMC guide identifies ESD as a key immunity factor used in EU EMC conformity assessment, describing it as protection against discharges from human contact or charged objects.

entries.topic: Radiated immunity as an EMC test family
entries.source_title: EMC Directive 2014/30/EU: Compliance, CE Marking & Testing
entries.brief_summary: Accredited-lab EMC guide identifies radiated immunity as resistance to surrounding electromagnetic fields and treats it as a core EU EMC immunity topic alongside ESD and other disturbance tests.

regulations.title: Regulations
regulations.description: Overview of reliability and airworthiness regulations.

subsections.name: Reliability and Airworthiness Regulations
subsections.summary: Reliability and Airworthiness Regulations for UAV systems

entries.brief_summary: The low voltage directive (LVD) (2014/35/EU) ensures that electrical equipment within certain voltage limits provides a high level of protection for European citizens, and benefits fully from the single market.

regulations.title: Regulations
regulations.description: Overview of cybersecurity regulations in Taiwan

subsections.name: Cybersecurity Regulations
subsections.summary: Cybersecurity Regulations for UAV systems in the EU

entries.brief_summary: This Regulation aims to set the boundary conditions for the development of secure products with digital elements by ensuring that hardware and software products are placed on the market with fewer vulnerabilities and that manufacturers take security seriously throughout a product’s lifecycle. It also aims to create conditions allowing users to take cybersecurity into account when selecting and using products with digital elements, for example by improving transparency with regard to the support period for products with digital elements made available on the market.

This Directive lays down measures that aim to achieve a high common level of cybersecurity across the Union, with a view to improving the functioning of the internal market. By 17 October 2024, Member States shall adopt and publish the measures necessary to comply with this Directive. They shall immediately inform the Commission thereof.

entries.topic: Regulation EU 2019/881 AKA the Cybersecurity Act
entries.brief_summary: Regulation (EU) 2019/881 aims to achieve a high level of cybersecurity, cyber resilience and trust in the European Union (EU) by setting out objectives, tasks and organisational matters for a strengthened and renamed European Union Agency for Cybersecurity (ENISA), with a new permanent mandate; a framework for voluntary European cybersecurity certification schemes for information and communications technology (ICT) products, services and processes, and managed security services.

The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) is the toughest privacy and security law in the world. Though it was drafted and passed by the European Union (EU), it imposes obligations onto organizations anywhere, so long as they target or collect data related to people in the EU.
